authors_text: Garrett Thomas,Ching-An Cheng,Ricky Loynd,Felipe Vieira Frujeri,Vibhav Vineet,Mihai Jalobeanu,Andrey Kolobov
title: PLEX: Making the Most of the Available Data for Robotic Manipulation Pretraining
title_zh: PLEX：充分利用机器人操作预训练的可用数据
abstract: A rich representation is key to general robotic manipulation, but existing approaches to representation learning require large amounts of multimodal demonstrations. In this work we propose PLEX, a transformer-based architecture that learns from a small amount of task-agnostic visuomotor trajectories and a much larger amount of task-conditioned object manipulation videos -- a type of data available in quantity. PLEX uses visuomotor trajectories to induce a latent feature space and to learn task-agnostic manipulation routines, while diverse video-only demonstrations teach PLEX how to plan in the induced latent feature space for a wide variety of tasks. Experiments showcase PLEX's generalization on Meta-World and SOTA performance in challenging Robosuite environments. In particular, using relative positional encoding in PLEX's transformers greatly helps in low-data regimes of learning from human-collected demonstrations. The paper's accompanying code and data are available at https://microsoft.github.io/PLEX.
summary: A rich representation is key to general robotic manipulation, but existing approaches to representation learning require large amounts of multimodal demonstrations. In this work we propose PLEX, a transformer-based architecture that learns from a small amount of task-agnostic visuomotor trajectories and a much larger amount of task-conditioned object manipulation videos -- a type of data available in quantity. PLEX uses visuomotor trajectories to induce a latent feature space and to learn task-agnostic manipulation routines, while diverse video-only demonstrations teach PLEX how to plan in the induced latent feature space for a wide variety of tasks. Experiments showcase PLEX's generalization on Meta-World and SOTA performance in challenging Robosuite environments. In particular, using relative positional encoding in PLEX's transformers greatly helps in low-data regimes of learning from human-collected demonstrations. The paper's accompanying code and data are available at https://microsoft.github.io/PLEX.
abstract_zh: 丰富的表示是一般机器人操作的关键，但现有的表示学习方法需要大量的多模式演示。在这项工作中，我们提出了 PLEX，一种基于 Transformer 的架构，可以从少量与任务无关的视觉运动轨迹和大量任务条件对象操作视频（一种大量可用的数据）中学习。 PLEX 使用视觉运动轨迹来诱导潜在特征空间并学习与任务无关的操作例程，而各种仅视频演示则教 PLEX 如何在诱导的潜在特征空间中为各种任务进行规划。实验展示了 PLEX 在具有挑战性的 Robosuite 环境中对 Meta-World 和 SOTA 性能的概括。特别是，在 PLEX 的转换器中使用相对位置编码极大地有助于从人类收集的演示中进行学习的低数据状态。该论文附带的代码和数据可在 https://microsoft.github.io/PLEX 上获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PLEX，一种基于Transformer的机器人操作预训练框架，通过结合少量任务无关的视觉运动轨迹和大量任务相关视频数据，提升机器人操作的泛化能力。
AI.motivation: 现有机器人操作表示学习方法依赖大量多模态演示数据，而实际中高质量数据获取困难，需探索更高效利用可用数据的方法。
AI.method: PLEX采用Transformer架构，利用少量视觉运动轨迹构建潜在特征空间并学习通用操作模式，同时通过大量视频数据学习多样化任务的规划策略，并引入相对位置编码增强低数据场景下的学习效果。
AI.result: 在Meta-World基准测试中实现良好泛化，在Robosuite复杂环境中达到SOTA性能，尤其在人类演示数据量较少时表现显著优于基线方法。
AI.conclusion: PLEX通过有效融合不同数据模态和结构设计，在少样本场景下显著提升了机器人操作预训练的效率和效果。

The paper's accompanying code and data are available at https://microsoft.github.io/PLEX. 丰富的表示是一般机器人操作的关键，但现有的表示学习方法需要大量的多模式演示。在这项工作中，我们提出了 PLEX，一种基于 Transformer 的架构，可以从少量与任务无关的视觉运动轨迹和大量任务条件对象操作视频（一种大量可用的数据）中学习。 PLEX 使用视觉运动轨迹来诱导潜在特征空间并学习与任务无关的操作例程，而各种仅视频演示则教 PLEX 如何在诱导的潜在特征空间中为各种任务进行规划。实验展示了 PLEX 在具有挑战性的 Robosuite 环境中对 Meta-World 和 SOTA 性能的概括。特别是，在 PLEX 的转换器中使用相对位置编码极大地有助于从人类收集的演示中进行学习的低数据状态。该论文附带的代码和数据可在 https://microsoft.github.io/PLEX 上获取。 本文提出PLEX，一种基于Transformer的机器人操作预训练框架，通过结合少量任务无关的视觉运动轨迹和大量任务相关视频数据，提升机器人操作的泛化能力。 Garrett Thomas,Ching-An Cheng,Ricky Loynd,Felipe Vieira Frujeri,Vibhav Vineet,Mihai Jalobeanu,Andrey Kolobov cs.RO,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Aditay Tripathi,Anand Mishra,Anirban Chakraborty
title: Query-guided Attention in Vision Transformers for Localizing Objects Using a Single Sketch
title_zh: 视觉变换器中的查询引导注意力，用于使用单个草图本地化对象
abstract: In this work, we investigate the problem of sketch-based object localization on natural images, where given a crude hand-drawn sketch of an object, the goal is to localize all the instances of the same object on the target image. This problem proves difficult due to the abstract nature of hand-drawn sketches, variations in the style and quality of sketches, and the large domain gap existing between the sketches and the natural images. To mitigate these challenges, existing works proposed attention-based frameworks to incorporate query information into the image features. However, in these works, the query features are incorporated after the image features have already been independently learned, leading to inadequate alignment. In contrast, we propose a sketch-guided vision transformer encoder that uses cross-attention after each block of the transformer-based image encoder to learn query-conditioned image features leading to stronger alignment with the query sketch. Further, at the output of the decoder, the object and the sketch features are refined to bring the representation of relevant objects closer to the sketch query and thereby improve the localization. The proposed model also generalizes to the object categories not seen during training, as the target image features learned by our method are query-aware. Our localization framework can also utilize multiple sketch queries via a trainable novel sketch fusion strategy. The model is evaluated on the images from the public object detection benchmark, namely MS-COCO, using the sketch queries from QuickDraw! and Sketchy datasets. Compared with existing localization methods, the proposed approach gives a $6.6\%$ and $8.0\%$ improvement in mAP for seen objects using sketch queries from QuickDraw! and Sketchy datasets, respectively, and a $12.2\%$ improvement in AP@50 for large objects that are `unseen' during training.
summary: In this work, we investigate the problem of sketch-based object localization on natural images, where given a crude hand-drawn sketch of an object, the goal is to localize all the instances of the same object on the target image. This problem proves difficult due to the abstract nature of hand-drawn sketches, variations in the style and quality of sketches, and the large domain gap existing between the sketches and the natural images. To mitigate these challenges, existing works proposed attention-based frameworks to incorporate query information into the image features. However, in these works, the query features are incorporated after the image features have already been independently learned, leading to inadequate alignment. In contrast, we propose a sketch-guided vision transformer encoder that uses cross-attention after each block of the transformer-based image encoder to learn query-conditioned image features leading to stronger alignment with the query sketch. Further, at the output of the decoder, the object and the sketch features are refined to bring the representation of relevant objects closer to the sketch query and thereby improve the localization. The proposed model also generalizes to the object categories not seen during training, as the target image features learned by our method are query-aware. Our localization framework can also utilize multiple sketch queries via a trainable novel sketch fusion strategy. The model is evaluated on the images from the public object detection benchmark, namely MS-COCO, using the sketch queries from QuickDraw! and Sketchy datasets. Compared with existing localization methods, the proposed approach gives a $6.6\%$ and $8.0\%$ improvement in mAP for seen objects using sketch queries from QuickDraw! and Sketchy datasets, respectively, and a $12.2\%$ improvement in AP@50 for large objects that are `unseen' during training.
abstract_zh: 在这项工作中，我们研究了自然图像上基于草图的对象定位问题，其中给定一个对象的粗略手绘草图，目标是在目标图像上定位同一对象的所有实例。由于手绘草图的抽象性质、草图的风格和质量的变化以及草图和自然图像之间存在较大的域差距，这个问题被证明是困难的。为了缓解这些挑战，现有的工作提出了基于注意力的框架，将查询信息合并到图像特征中。然而，在这些工作中，查询特征是在图像特征已经独立学习之后才被合并的，导致对齐不充分。相比之下，我们提出了一种草图引导的视觉变换器编码器，它在基于变换器的图像编码器的每个块之后使用交叉注意力来学习查询条件图像特征，从而与查询草图更强地对齐。此外，在解码器的输出处，对象和草图特征被细化，以使相关对象的表示更接近草图查询，从而改善定位。所提出的模型还推广到训练期间未见过的对象类别，因为我们的方法学习的目标图像特征是查询感知的。我们的本地化框架还可以通过可训练的新颖草图融合策略来利用多个草图查询。该模型使用来自 QuickDraw 的草图查询，在公共对象检测基准（即 MS-COCO）的图像上进行评估！和粗略的数据集。与现有的定位方法相比，所提出的方法使用 QuickDraw 中的草图查询对可见对象的 mAP 提高了 $6.6\%$ 和 $8.0\%$！和 Sketchy 数据集，对于训练期间“看不见”的大型物体，AP@50 提高了 12.2\%$。

AI.tldr: 本文研究如何利用手绘草图在自然图像中定位目标对象，提出了一种基于视觉Transformer的查询引导注意力机制以提升定位效果。
AI.motivation: 手绘草图存在抽象性、风格差异及与自然图像的领域差异，导致现有方法在融合查询信息时对齐不足，难以有效定位目标。
AI.method: 设计了一种草图引导的视觉Transformer编码器，在每个Transformer块后使用交叉注意力学习查询条件下的图像特征，并在解码器输出端优化对象与草图特征表示，同时引入可训练的多草图融合策略。
AI.result: 在MS-COCO数据集上，使用QuickDraw!和Sketchy数据集的草图查询，对已见对象的mAP分别提升6.6%和8.0%，对训练时未见的大尺寸对象AP@50提升12.2%。
AI.conclusion: 通过早期融合查询信息和特征优化，该方法增强了草图与图像的对齐效果，能够泛化到未见类别并支持多草图输入，显著提升了单草图定位性能。

authors_text: Federico Paredes-Vallés,Jesse Hagenaars,Julien Dupeyroux,Stein Stroobants,Yingfu Xu,Guido de Croon
title: Fully neuromorphic vision and control for autonomous drone flight
title_zh: 用于自主无人机飞行的完全神经拟态视觉和控制
abstract: Biological sensing and processing is asynchronous and sparse, leading to low-latency and energy-efficient perception and action. In robotics, neuromorphic hardware for event-based vision and spiking neural networks promises to exhibit similar characteristics. However, robotic implementations have been limited to basic tasks with low-dimensional sensory inputs and motor actions due to the restricted network size in current embedded neuromorphic processors and the difficulties of training spiking neural networks. Here, we present the first fully neuromorphic vision-to-control pipeline for controlling a freely flying drone. Specifically, we train a spiking neural network that accepts high-dimensional raw event-based camera data and outputs low-level control actions for performing autonomous vision-based flight. The vision part of the network, consisting of five layers and 28.8k neurons, maps incoming raw events to ego-motion estimates and is trained with self-supervised learning on real event data. The control part consists of a single decoding layer and is learned with an evolutionary algorithm in a drone simulator. Robotic experiments show a successful sim-to-real transfer of the fully learned neuromorphic pipeline. The drone can accurately follow different ego-motion setpoints, allowing for hovering, landing, and maneuvering sideways$\unicode{x2014}$even while yawing at the same time. The neuromorphic pipeline runs on board on Intel's Loihi neuromorphic processor with an execution frequency of 200 Hz, spending only 27 $\unicode{x00b5}$J per inference. These results illustrate the potential of neuromorphic sensing and processing for enabling smaller, more intelligent robots.
summary: Biological sensing and processing is asynchronous and sparse, leading to low-latency and energy-efficient perception and action. In robotics, neuromorphic hardware for event-based vision and spiking neural networks promises to exhibit similar characteristics. However, robotic implementations have been limited to basic tasks with low-dimensional sensory inputs and motor actions due to the restricted network size in current embedded neuromorphic processors and the difficulties of training spiking neural networks. Here, we present the first fully neuromorphic vision-to-control pipeline for controlling a freely flying drone. Specifically, we train a spiking neural network that accepts high-dimensional raw event-based camera data and outputs low-level control actions for performing autonomous vision-based flight. The vision part of the network, consisting of five layers and 28.8k neurons, maps incoming raw events to ego-motion estimates and is trained with self-supervised learning on real event data. The control part consists of a single decoding layer and is learned with an evolutionary algorithm in a drone simulator. Robotic experiments show a successful sim-to-real transfer of the fully learned neuromorphic pipeline. The drone can accurately follow different ego-motion setpoints, allowing for hovering, landing, and maneuvering sideways$\unicode{x2014}$even while yawing at the same time. The neuromorphic pipeline runs on board on Intel's Loihi neuromorphic processor with an execution frequency of 200 Hz, spending only 27 $\unicode{x00b5}$J per inference. These results illustrate the potential of neuromorphic sensing and processing for enabling smaller, more intelligent robots.
abstract_zh: 生物传感和处理是异步和稀疏的，从而实现低延迟和节能的感知和行动。在机器人技术中，用于基于事件的视觉和尖峰神经网络的神经形态硬件有望表现出类似的特征。然而，由于当前嵌入式神经形态处理器的网络大小有限以及训练尖峰神经网络的困难，机器人的实现仅限于具有低维感觉输入和运动动作的基本任务。在这里，我们提出了第一个完全神经形态视觉控制管道，用于控制自由飞行的无人机。具体来说，我们训练一个尖峰神经网络，该网络接受高维的基于事件的原始相机数据并输出低级控制动作以执行基于视觉的自主飞行。网络的视觉部分由五层和 28.8k 个神经元组成，将传入的原始事件映射到自我运动估计，并通过真实事件数据的自我监督学习进行训练。控制部分由单个解码层组成，并通过无人机模拟器中的进化算法进行学习。机器人实验表明，完全学习的神经形态管道成功地从模拟到真实的迁移。无人机可以准确地遵循不同的自我运动设定点，允许悬停、着陆和侧向操纵$\unicode{x2014}$，甚至在偏航的同时也是如此。神经形态管道在英特尔 Loihi 神经形态处理器上运行，执行频率为 200 Hz，每次推理仅花费 27 $\unicode{x00b5}$J。这些结果说明了神经形态传感和处理在实现更小、更智能的机器人方面的潜力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出首个完全基于神经形态计算的视觉到控制流水线，实现自主飞行无人机的实时控制。
AI.motivation: 生物感知具有异步性和稀疏性特征，能实现低延迟和高能效的感知与行动，但现有神经形态硬件因网络规模限制和训练难度，仅能处理低维任务。
AI.method: 训练包含5层28.8k神经元的脉冲神经网络处理事件相机数据，通过自监督学习估计运动状态；控制部分采用进化算法在模拟器中训练，最终在Intel Loihi芯片上实现端到端运行。
AI.result: 无人机成功实现仿真到实物的迁移，可精准跟踪运动指令完成悬停、降落和侧向机动，单次推理耗能仅27µJ，运行频率达200Hz。
AI.conclusion: 神经形态感知与处理技术为小型智能机器人提供了低功耗、高实时性的解决方案。

result: 无人机成功实现仿真到实物的迁移，可精准跟踪运动指令完成悬停、降落和侧向机动，单次推理耗能仅27µJ，运行频率达200Hz。
conclusion: 神经形态感知与处理技术为小型智能机器人提供了低功耗、高实时性的解决方案。
search_text: Fully neuromorphic vision and control for autonomous drone flight 用于自主无人机飞行的完全神经拟态视觉和控制 Biological sensing and processing is asynchronous and sparse, leading to low-latency and energy-efficient perception and action. In robotics, neuromorphic hardware for event-based vision and spiking neural networks promises to exhibit similar characteristics. However, robotic implementations have been limited to basic tasks with low-dimensional sensory inputs and motor actions due to the restricted network size in current embedded neuromorphic processors and the difficulties of training spiking neural networks. Here, we present the first fully neuromorphic vision-to-control pipeline for controlling a freely flying drone. Specifically, we train a spiking neural network that accepts high-dimensional raw event-based camera data and outputs low-level control actions for performing autonomous vision-based flight. The vision part of the network, consisting of five layers and 28.8k neurons, maps incoming raw events to ego-motion estimates and is trained with self-supervised learning on real event data. The control part consists of a single decoding layer and is learned with an evolutionary algorithm in a drone simulator. Robotic experiments show a successful sim-to-real transfer of the fully learned neuromorphic pipeline. The drone can accurately follow different ego-motion setpoints, allowing for hovering, landing, and maneuvering sideways$\unicode{x2014}$even while yawing at the same time. The neuromorphic pipeline runs on board on Intel's Loihi neuromorphic processor with an execution frequency of 200 Hz, spending only 27 $\unicode{x00b5}$J per inference. These results illustrate the potential of neuromorphic sensing and processing for enabling smaller, more intelligent robots. 生物传感和处理是异步和稀疏的，从而实现低延迟和节能的感知和行动。在机器人技术中，用于基于事件的视觉和尖峰神经网络的神经形态硬件有望表现出类似的特征。然而，由于当前嵌入式神经形态处理器的网络大小有限以及训练尖峰神经网络的困难，机器人的实现仅限于具有低维感觉输入和运动动作的基本任务。在这里，我们提出了第一个完全神经形态视觉控制管道，用于控制自由飞行的无人机。具体来说，我们训练一个尖峰神经网络，该网络接受高维的基于事件的原始相机数据并输出低级控制动作以执行基于视觉的自主飞行。网络的视觉部分由五层和 28.8k 个神经元组成，将传入的原始事件映射到自我运动估计，并通过真实事件数据的自我监督学习进行训练。控制部分由单个解码层组成，并通过无人机模拟器中的进化算法进行学习。机器人实验表明，完全学习的神经形态管道成功地从模拟到真实的迁移。无人机可以准确地遵循不同的自我运动设定点，允许悬停、着陆和侧向操纵$\unicode{x2014}$，甚至在偏航的同时也是如此。神经形态管道在英特尔 Loihi 神经形态处理器上运行，执行频率为 200 Hz，每次推理仅花费 27 $\unicode{x00b5}$J。这些结果说明了神经形态传感和处理在实现更小、更智能的机器人方面的潜力。 本文提出首个完全基于神经形态计算的视觉到控制流水线，实现自主飞行无人机的实时控制。 Federico Paredes-Vallés,Jesse Hagenaars,Julien Dupeyroux,Stein Stroobants,Yingfu Xu,Guido de Croon cs.RO,cs.AI,cs.CV,cs.LG,cs.NE

authors_text: OpenAI,Josh Achiam,Steven Adler,Sandhini Agarwal,Lama Ahmad,Ilge Akkaya,Florencia Leoni Aleman,Diogo Almeida,Janko Altenschmidt,Sam Altman,Shyamal Anadkat,Red Avila,Igor Babuschkin,Suchir Balaji,Valerie Balcom,Paul Baltescu,Haiming Bao,Mohammad Bavarian,Jeff Belgum,Irwan Bello,Jake Berdine,Gabriel Bernadett-Shapiro,Christopher Berner,Lenny Bogdonoff,Oleg Boiko, et al. title: GPT-4 Technical Report
title_zh: GPT-4 技术报告
comment: 100 pages; updated authors list; fixed author names and added citation
comments: 100 pages; updated authors list; fixed author names and added citation
abstract: We report the development of GPT-4, a large-scale, multimodal model which can accept image and text inputs and produce text outputs. While less capable than humans in many real-world scenarios, GPT-4 exhibits human-level performance on various professional and academic benchmarks, including passing a simulated bar exam with a score around the top 10% of test takers. GPT-4 is a Transformer-based model pre-trained to predict the next token in a document. The post-training alignment process results in improved performance on measures of factuality and adherence to desired behavior. A core component of this project was developing infrastructure and optimization methods that behave predictably across a wide range of scales. This allowed us to accurately predict some aspects of GPT-4's performance based on models trained with no more than 1/1,000th the compute of GPT-4.
summary: We report the development of GPT-4, a large-scale, multimodal model which can accept image and text inputs and produce text outputs. While less capable than humans in many real-world scenarios, GPT-4 exhibits human-level performance on various professional and academic benchmarks, including passing a simulated bar exam with a score around the top 10% of test takers. GPT-4 is a Transformer-based model pre-trained to predict the next token in a document. The post-training alignment process results in improved performance on measures of factuality and adherence to desired behavior. A core component of this project was developing infrastructure and optimization methods that behave predictably across a wide range of scales. This allowed us to accurately predict some aspects of GPT-4's performance based on models trained with no more than 1/1,000th the compute of GPT-4.
abstract_zh: 我们报告了 GPT-4 的开发，这是一种大规模、多模式模型，可以接受图像和文本输入并生成文本输出。虽然在许多现实场景中，GPT-4 的能力不如人类，但它在各种专业和学术基准上展现了人类水平的表现，包括通过模拟律师考试，分数在考生中排名前 10%。 GPT-4 是一个基于 Transformer 的模型，经过预先训练，用于预测文档中的下一个标记。培训后的调整过程可以提高事实性衡量标准和对期望行为的遵守程度。该项目的核心组成部分是开发基础设施和优化方法，使其在广泛的尺度上表现可预测。这使我们能够基于使用不超过 GPT-4 1/1,000 计算量训练的模型来准确预测 GPT-4 性能的某些方面。

AI.tldr: 介绍GPT-4的开发，这是一种大规模多模态模型，能够处理图像和文本输入并生成文本输出，在多个专业和学术基准测试中达到人类水平。
AI.motivation: 推动模型在复杂任务和专业领域中的性能提升，通过多模态处理和优化方法实现更广泛的应用。
AI.method: 基于Transformer架构的预训练模型，结合后训练对齐优化，并开发了跨规模的基础设施和优化方法以确保性能可预测性。
AI.result: GPT-4在多个专业和学术基准测试中达到人类水平，例如模拟律师考试成绩进入前10%，并成功预测了部分性能指标。
AI.conclusion: 通过多模态处理和优化方法，GPT-4在复杂任务中表现出色，展示了大规模模型在专业领域应用的潜力，并为未来模型开发提供了基础设施支持。

authors_text: Inhwa Han,Serin Yang,Taesung Kwon,Jong Chul Ye
title: Highly Personalized Text Embedding for Image Manipulation by Stable Diffusion
title_zh: 通过稳定扩散进行图像操作的高度个性化文本嵌入
abstract: Diffusion models have shown superior performance in image generation and manipulation, but the inherent stochasticity presents challenges in preserving and manipulating image content and identity. While previous approaches like DreamBooth and Textual Inversion have proposed model or latent representation personalization to maintain the content, their reliance on multiple reference images and complex training limits their practicality. In this paper, we present a simple yet highly effective approach to personalization using highly personalized (HiPer) text embedding by decomposing the CLIP embedding space for personalization and content manipulation. Our method does not require model fine-tuning or identifiers, yet still enables manipulation of background, texture, and motion with just a single image and target text. Through experiments on diverse target texts, we demonstrate that our approach produces highly personalized and complex semantic image edits across a wide range of tasks. We believe that the novel understanding of the text embedding space presented in this work has the potential to inspire further research across various tasks.
summary: Diffusion models have shown superior performance in image generation and manipulation, but the inherent stochasticity presents challenges in preserving and manipulating image content and identity. While previous approaches like DreamBooth and Textual Inversion have proposed model or latent representation personalization to maintain the content, their reliance on multiple reference images and complex training limits their practicality. In this paper, we present a simple yet highly effective approach to personalization using highly personalized (HiPer) text embedding by decomposing the CLIP embedding space for personalization and content manipulation. Our method does not require model fine-tuning or identifiers, yet still enables manipulation of background, texture, and motion with just a single image and target text. Through experiments on diverse target texts, we demonstrate that our approach produces highly personalized and complex semantic image edits across a wide range of tasks. We believe that the novel understanding of the text embedding space presented in this work has the potential to inspire further research across various tasks.
abstract_zh: 扩散模型在图像生成和操作方面表现出了卓越的性能，但其固有的随机性给保存和操作图像内容和身份带来了挑战。虽然 DreamBooth 和 Textual Inversion 等之前的方法提出了模型或潜在表示个性化来维护内容，但它们对多个参考图像和复杂训练的依赖限制了它们的实用性。在本文中，我们通过分解用于个性化和内容操作的 CLIP 嵌入空间，提出了一种使用高度个性化 (HiPer) 文本嵌入的简单但高效的个性化方法。我们的方法不需要模型微调或标识符，但仍然可以仅使用单个图像和目标文本来操纵背景、纹理和运动。通过对不同目标文本的实验，我们证明了我们的方法可以在广泛的任务中产生高度个性化和复杂的语义图像编辑。我们相信，这项工作中提出的对文本嵌入空间的新颖理解有可能激发跨各种任务的进一步研究。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无需模型微调的高效图像编辑方法，通过分解CLIP嵌入空间实现基于单张图像和目标文本的个性化文本嵌入，用于Stable Diffusion的图像操控。
AI.motivation: 现有方法如DreamBooth和Textual Inversion需依赖多张参考图像和复杂训练流程，限制了实际应用。本文旨在提供一种更简单且无需标识符的个性化方案。
AI.method: 通过分解CLIP嵌入空间，利用高度个性化（HiPer）文本嵌入实现内容操控，仅需单张图像和目标文本即可调整背景、纹理和运动，无需模型微调。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在多样化文本任务中可生成高度个性化且语义复杂的图像编辑结果，验证了其有效性。
AI.conclusion: 本文对文本嵌入空间的新理解为跨任务研究提供了潜在方向，推动了图像生成与操控领域的发展。

authors_text: Luca Tomasetti,Kjersti Engan,Liv Jorunn Høllesli,Kathinka Dæhli Kurz,Mahdieh Khanmohammadi
title: CT Perfusion is All We Need: 4D CNN Segmentation of Penumbra and Core in Patients With Suspected Ischemic Stroke
title_zh: CT 灌注就是我们所需要的：疑似缺血性中风患者的半影和核心的 4D CNN 分割
abstract: Precise and fast prediction methods for ischemic areas comprised of dead tissue, core, and salvageable tissue, penumbra, in acute ischemic stroke (AIS) patients are of significant clinical interest. They play an essential role in improving diagnosis and treatment planning. Computed Tomography (CT) scan is one of the primary modalities for early assessment in patients with suspected AIS. CT Perfusion (CTP) is often used as a primary assessment to determine stroke location, severity, and volume of ischemic lesions. Current automatic segmentation methods for CTP mostly use already processed 3D parametric maps conventionally used for clinical interpretation by radiologists as input. Alternatively, the raw CTP data is used on a slice-by-slice basis as 2D+time input, where the spatial information over the volume is ignored. In addition, these methods are only interested in segmenting core regions, while predicting penumbra can be essential for treatment planning. This paper investigates different methods to utilize the entire 4D CTP as input to fully exploit the spatio-temporal information, leading us to propose a novel 4D convolution layer. Our comprehensive experiments on a local dataset of 152 patients divided into three groups show that our proposed models generate more precise results than other methods explored. Adopting the proposed 4D mJ-Net, a Dice Coefficient of 0.53 and 0.23 is achieved for segmenting penumbra and core areas, respectively. The code is available on https://github.com/Biomedical-Data-Analysis-Laboratory/4D-mJ-Net.git.
summary: Precise and fast prediction methods for ischemic areas comprised of dead tissue, core, and salvageable tissue, penumbra, in acute ischemic stroke (AIS) patients are of significant clinical interest. They play an essential role in improving diagnosis and treatment planning. Computed Tomography (CT) scan is one of the primary modalities for early assessment in patients with suspected AIS. CT Perfusion (CTP) is often used as a primary assessment to determine stroke location, severity, and volume of ischemic lesions. Alternatively, the raw CTP data is used on a slice-by-slice basis as 2D+time input, where the spatial information over the volume is ignored. In addition, these methods are only interested in segmenting core regions, while predicting penumbra can be essential for treatment planning. This paper investigates different methods to utilize the entire 4D CTP as input to fully exploit the spatio-temporal information, leading us to propose a novel 4D convolution layer. Our comprehensive experiments on a local dataset of 152 patients divided into three groups show that our proposed models generate more precise results than other methods explored. Adopting the proposed 4D mJ-Net, a Dice Coefficient of 0.53 and 0.23 is achieved for segmenting penumbra and core areas, respectively. The code is available on https://github.com/Biomedical-Data-Analysis-Laboratory/4D-mJ-Net.git.
abstract_zh: 急性缺血性中风（AIS）患者的缺血区域（包括死亡组织、核心和可挽救组织、半暗带）的精确、快速预测方法具有重要的临床意义。它们在改善诊断和治疗计划方面发挥着重要作用。计算机断层扫描 (CT) 扫描是对疑似 AIS 患者进行早期评估的主要方式之一。 CT 灌注 (CTP) 通常用作确定中风位置、严重程度和缺血性病变体积的主要评估。当前的 CTP 自动分割方法大多使用已处理的 3D 参数图，通常由放射科医生用于临床解释作为输入。或者，原始 CTP 数据在逐片的基础上用作 2D+时间输入，其中体积上的空间信息被忽略。此外，这些方法只对分割核心区域感兴趣，而预测半影对于治疗计划至关重要。本文研究了利用整个 4D CTP 作为输入来充分利用时空信息的不同方法，从而提出了一种新颖的 4D 卷积层。我们对分为三组的 152 名患者的本地数据集进行综合实验表明，我们提出的模型比其他探索的方法产生更精确的结果。采用所提出的 4D mJ-Net，分割半影和核心区域的 Dice 系数分别为 0.53 和 0.23。该代码可在 https://github.com/Biomedical-Data-Analysis-Laboratory/4D-mJ-Net.git 上获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于4D卷积神经网络（4D CNN）的方法，用于急性缺血性卒中患者的梗死核心和可挽救组织（半暗带）的分割。
AI.motivation: 现有方法多使用预处理的3D参数图或逐层2D+时间数据，忽略了空间信息，且主要关注核心区域而忽视对治疗规划关键的半暗带预测。
AI.method: 设计了一种新型4D卷积层，充分利用CT灌注（CTP）的时空信息，并提出4D mJ-Net模型进行分割。
AI.result: 在152例患者数据集上，4D mJ-Net模型的半暗带Dice系数达0.53，核心区域达0.23，优于其他方法。
AI.conclusion: 利用完整4D CTP数据和新型卷积层可显著提升卒中区域分割精度，为临床治疗规划提供支持。

authors_text: Akhil Meethal,Eric Granger,Marco Pedersoli
title: Cascaded Zoom-in Detector for High Resolution Aerial Images
title_zh: 用于高分辨率航空图像的级联放大探测器
abstract: Detecting objects in aerial images is challenging because they are typically composed of crowded small objects distributed non-uniformly over high-resolution images. Density cropping is a widely used method to improve this small object detection where the crowded small object regions are extracted and processed in high resolution. However, this is typically accomplished by adding other learnable components, thus complicating the training and inference over a standard detection process. In this paper, we propose an efficient Cascaded Zoom-in (CZ) detector that re-purposes the detector itself for density-guided training and inference. During training, density crops are located, labeled as a new class, and employed to augment the training dataset. During inference, the density crops are first detected along with the base class objects, and then input for a second stage of inference. This approach is easily integrated into any detector, and creates no significant change in the standard detection process, like the uniform cropping approach popular in aerial image detection. Experimental results on the aerial images of the challenging VisDrone and DOTA datasets verify the benefits of the proposed approach. The proposed CZ detector also provides state-of-the-art results over uniform cropping and other density cropping methods on the VisDrone dataset, increasing the detection mAP of small objects by more than 3 points.
summary: Detecting objects in aerial images is challenging because they are typically composed of crowded small objects distributed non-uniformly over high-resolution images. Density cropping is a widely used method to improve this small object detection where the crowded small object regions are extracted and processed in high resolution. However, this is typically accomplished by adding other learnable components, thus complicating the training and inference over a standard detection process. In this paper, we propose an efficient Cascaded Zoom-in (CZ) detector that re-purposes the detector itself for density-guided training and inference. During training, density crops are located, labeled as a new class, and employed to augment the training dataset. During inference, the density crops are first detected along with the base class objects, and then input for a second stage of inference. This approach is easily integrated into any detector, and creates no significant change in the standard detection process, like the uniform cropping approach popular in aerial image detection. Experimental results on the aerial images of the challenging VisDrone and DOTA datasets verify the benefits of the proposed approach. The proposed CZ detector also provides state-of-the-art results over uniform cropping and other density cropping methods on the VisDrone dataset, increasing the detection mAP of small objects by more than 3 points.
abstract_zh: 检测航空图像中的物体具有挑战性，因为它们通常由在高分辨率图像上不均匀分布的拥挤小物体组成。密度裁剪是一种广泛使用的方法来改进这种小物体检测，其中拥挤的小物体区域被提取并以高分辨率进行处理。然而，这通常是通过添加其他可学习组件来实现的，从而使标准检测过程的训练和推理变得复杂。在本文中，我们提出了一种高效的级联放大（CZ）检测器，它将检测器本身重新用于密度引导的训练和推理。在训练期间，密度作物被定位，标记为新类别，并用于增强训练数据集。在推理过程中，首先检测密度作物以及基类对象，然后输入推理的第二阶段。这种方法可以轻松集成到任何检测器中，并且不会对标准检测过程造成重大变化，就像航空图像检测中流行的均匀裁剪方法一样。具有挑战性的 VisDrone 和 DOTA 数据集的航拍图像的实验结果验证了所提出方法的优点。所提出的 CZ 检测器还在 VisDrone 数据集上提供了比均匀裁剪和其他密度裁剪方法更先进的结果，将小物体的检测 mAP 提高了 3 个点以上。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种级联缩放检测器（CZ），用于解决高分辨率航拍图像中小物体检测问题。
AI.motivation: 现有密度裁剪方法需添加额外组件，导致训练和推理过程复杂化，亟需更高效的解决方案。
AI.method: 通过重新利用检测器自身实现密度引导的训练与推理，训练时将密度裁剪区域标记为新类别增强数据集，推理时分两阶段处理密度裁剪区域与基础类别物体。
AI.result: 在VisDrone和DOTA数据集上验证有效性，VisDrone数据集上小物体检测mAP提升超3点，优于均匀裁剪及其他密度裁剪方法。
AI.conclusion: CZ检测器以简洁方式集成至现有检测框架，在保持标准检测流程的同时显著提升小物体检测性能。

authors_text: Mohammed Abouheaf,Derek Boase,Wail Gueaieb,Davide Spinello,Salah Al-Sharhan
title: Real-Time Measurement-Driven Reinforcement Learning Control Approach for Uncertain Nonlinear Systems
title_zh: 不确定非线性系统的实时测量驱动的强化学习控制方法
abstract: The paper introduces an interactive machine learning mechanism to process the measurements of an uncertain, nonlinear dynamic process and hence advise an actuation strategy in real-time. For concept demonstration, a trajectory-following optimization problem of a Kinova robotic arm is solved using an integral reinforcement learning approach with guaranteed stability for slowly varying dynamics. The solution is implemented using a model-free value iteration process to solve the integral temporal difference equations of the problem. The performance of the proposed technique is benchmarked against that of another model-free high-order approach and is validated for dynamic payload and disturbances. Unlike its benchmark, the proposed adaptive strategy is capable of handling extreme process variations. This is experimentally demonstrated by introducing static and time-varying payloads close to the rated maximum payload capacity of the manipulator arm. The comparison algorithm exhibited up to a seven-fold percent overshoot compared to the proposed integral reinforcement learning solution. The robustness of the algorithm is further validated by disturbing the real-time adapted strategy gains with a white noise of a standard deviation as high as 5%.
summary: The paper introduces an interactive machine learning mechanism to process the measurements of an uncertain, nonlinear dynamic process and hence advise an actuation strategy in real-time. For concept demonstration, a trajectory-following optimization problem of a Kinova robotic arm is solved using an integral reinforcement learning approach with guaranteed stability for slowly varying dynamics. The solution is implemented using a model-free value iteration process to solve the integral temporal difference equations of the problem. The performance of the proposed technique is benchmarked against that of another model-free high-order approach and is validated for dynamic payload and disturbances. Unlike its benchmark, the proposed adaptive strategy is capable of handling extreme process variations. This is experimentally demonstrated by introducing static and time-varying payloads close to the rated maximum payload capacity of the manipulator arm. The comparison algorithm exhibited up to a seven-fold percent overshoot compared to the proposed integral reinforcement learning solution. The robustness of the algorithm is further validated by disturbing the real-time adapted strategy gains with a white noise of a standard deviation as high as 5%.
abstract_zh: 该论文引入了一种交互式机器学习机制来处理不确定的非线性动态过程的测量结果，从而实时建议驱动策略。为了进行概念演示，使用积分强化学习方法解决了 Kinova 机械臂的轨迹跟踪优化问题，并保证了缓慢变化动态的稳定性。该解决方案是使用无模型值迭代过程来求解问题的积分时间差分方程来实现的。所提出技术的性能以另一种无模型高阶方法的性能为基准，并针对动态有效负载和干扰进行了验证。与其基准不同，所提出的自适应策略能够处理极端的过程变化。通过引入接近机械臂额定最大有效负载能力的静态和时变有效负载，可以通过实验证明这一点。与所提出的积分强化学习解决方案相比，该比较算法的超调量高达七倍。通过用标准差高达5%的白噪声干扰实时自适应策略增益，进一步验证了算法的鲁棒性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于实时测量的强化学习控制方法，用于处理不确定非线性系统的动态过程，并通过Kinova机械臂轨迹跟踪问题验证了该方法的有效性。
AI.motivation: 现有控制方法在处理动态负载变化和外部干扰时存在稳定性不足的问题，尤其在非线性系统中难以保证实时性与鲁棒性。
AI.method: 采用模型无关的价值迭代算法求解积分时序差分方程，结合积分强化学习框架实现实时控制策略调整，并通过白噪声干扰测试验证算法鲁棒性。
AI.result: 实验表明，与基准方法相比，所提方法在动态负载和干扰下的超调量降低至基准的1/7，且在5%标准差白噪声干扰下仍保持稳定控制。
AI.conclusion: 该积分强化学习控制方法能有效应对非线性系统的不确定性，为实时动态环境下的鲁棒控制提供了新思路。

search_text: Real-Time Measurement-Driven Reinforcement Learning Control Approach for Uncertain Nonlinear Systems 不确定非线性系统的实时测量驱动的强化学习控制方法 The paper introduces an interactive machine learning mechanism to process the measurements of an uncertain, nonlinear dynamic process and hence advise an actuation strategy in real-time. For concept demonstration, a trajectory-following optimization problem of a Kinova robotic arm is solved using an integral reinforcement learning approach with guaranteed stability for slowly varying dynamics. The solution is implemented using a model-free value iteration process to solve the integral temporal difference equations of the problem. The performance of the proposed technique is benchmarked against that of another model-free high-order approach and is validated for dynamic payload and disturbances. Unlike its benchmark, the proposed adaptive strategy is capable of handling extreme process variations. This is experimentally demonstrated by introducing static and time-varying payloads close to the rated maximum payload capacity of the manipulator arm. The comparison algorithm exhibited up to a seven-fold percent overshoot compared to the proposed integral reinforcement learning solution. The robustness of the algorithm is further validated by disturbing the real-time adapted strategy gains with a white noise of a standard deviation as high as 5%. 该论文引入了一种交互式机器学习机制来处理不确定的非线性动态过程的测量结果，从而实时建议驱动策略。为了进行概念演示，使用积分强化学习方法解决了 Kinova 机械臂的轨迹跟踪优化问题，并保证了缓慢变化动态的稳定性。该解决方案是使用无模型值迭代过程来求解问题的积分时间差分方程来实现的。所提出技术的性能以另一种无模型高阶方法的性能为基准，并针对动态有效负载和干扰进行了验证。与其基准不同，所提出的自适应策略能够处理极端的过程变化。通过引入接近机械臂额定最大有效负载能力的静态和时变有效负载，可以通过实验证明这一点。与所提出的积分强化学习解决方案相比，该比较算法的超调量高达七倍。通过用标准差高达5%的白噪声干扰实时自适应策略增益，进一步验证了算法的鲁棒性。 本文提出了一种基于实时测量的强化学习控制方法，用于处理不确定非线性系统的动态过程，并通过Kinova机械臂轨迹跟踪问题验证了该方法的有效性。 Mohammed Abouheaf,Derek Boase,Wail Gueaieb,Davide Spinello,Salah Al-Sharhan eess.SY,cs.RO

authors_text: Simon Bilik,Daniel Batrakhanov,Tuomas Eerola,Lumi Haraguchi,Kaisa Kraft,Silke Van den Wyngaert,Jonna Kangas,Conny Sjöqvist,Karin Madsen,Lasse Lensu,Heikki Kälviäinen,Karel Horak
title: Towards Phytoplankton Parasite Detection Using Autoencoders
title_zh: 使用自动编码器进行浮游植物寄生虫检测
abstract: Phytoplankton parasites are largely understudied microbial components with a potentially significant ecological impact on phytoplankton bloom dynamics. To better understand their impact, we need improved detection methods to integrate phytoplankton parasite interactions in monitoring aquatic ecosystems. Automated imaging devices usually produce high amount of phytoplankton image data, while the occurrence of anomalous phytoplankton data is rare. Thus, we propose an unsupervised anomaly detection system based on the similarity of the original and autoencoder-reconstructed samples. With this approach, we were able to reach an overall F1 score of 0.75 in nine phytoplankton species, which could be further improved by species-specific fine-tuning. The proposed unsupervised approach was further compared with the supervised Faster R-CNN based object detector. With this supervised approach and the model trained on plankton species and anomalies, we were able to reach the highest F1 score of 0.86. However, the unsupervised approach is expected to be more universal as it can detect also unknown anomalies and it does not require any annotated anomalous data that may not be always available in sufficient quantities. Although other studies have dealt with plankton anomaly detection in terms of non-plankton particles, or air bubble detection, our paper is according to our best knowledge the first one which focuses on automated anomaly detection considering putative phytoplankton parasites or infections.
summary: Phytoplankton parasites are largely understudied microbial components with a potentially significant ecological impact on phytoplankton bloom dynamics. To better understand their impact, we need improved detection methods to integrate phytoplankton parasite interactions in monitoring aquatic ecosystems. Automated imaging devices usually produce high amount of phytoplankton image data, while the occurrence of anomalous phytoplankton data is rare. Thus, we propose an unsupervised anomaly detection system based on the similarity of the original and autoencoder-reconstructed samples. With this approach, we were able to reach an overall F1 score of 0.75 in nine phytoplankton species, which could be further improved by species-specific fine-tuning. The proposed unsupervised approach was further compared with the supervised Faster R-CNN based object detector. With this supervised approach and the model trained on plankton species and anomalies, we were able to reach the highest F1 score of 0.86. However, the unsupervised approach is expected to be more universal as it can detect also unknown anomalies and it does not require any annotated anomalous data that may not be always available in sufficient quantities. Although other studies have dealt with plankton anomaly detection in terms of non-plankton particles, or air bubble detection, our paper is according to our best knowledge the first one which focuses on automated anomaly detection considering putative phytoplankton parasites or infections.
abstract_zh: 浮游植物寄生虫在很大程度上是未被充分研究的微生物成分，对浮游植物水华动态具有潜在的重大生态影响。为了更好地了解其影响，我们需要改进检测方法，将浮游植物寄生虫相互作用纳入监测水生生态系统。自动成像设备通常会产生大量的浮游植物图像数据，而异常浮游植物数据的出现很少。因此，我们提出了一种基于原始样本和自动编码器重建样本的相似性的无监督异常检测系统。通过这种方法，我们能够在 9 种浮游植物物种中达到 0.75 的总体 F1 分数，并且可以通过针对特定物种的微调来进一步提高该分数。所提出的无监督方法与基于 Faster R-CNN 的有监督目标检测器进行了进一步比较。通过这种监督方法以及针对浮游生物物种和异常情况进行训练的模型，我们能够达到最高的 F1 分数 0.86。然而，无监督方法预计将更加普遍，因为它还可以检测未知异常，并且不需要任何可能并不总是有足够数量的带注释的异常数据。尽管其他研究涉及非浮游生物颗粒或气泡检测方面的浮游生物异常检测，但据我们所知，我们的论文是第一篇专注于考虑假定的浮游植物寄生虫或感染的自动异常检测的论文。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于自编码器的无监督异常检测系统，用于自动检测浮游植物寄生虫。
AI.motivation: 浮游植物寄生虫对浮游植物群落动态有潜在生态影响，但现有检测方法缺乏足够数据且难以整合到生态监测中。
AI.method: 通过比较原始样本与自编码器重建样本的相似性实现无监督检测，并与基于Faster R-CNN的监督方法进行对比。
AI.result: 无监督方法在九种浮游植物中达到0.75的F1分数，监督方法在相同数据上达到0.86；无监督方法无需标注异常数据且可检测未知异常。
AI.conclusion: 本文首次针对浮游植物寄生虫的自动化异常检测提出解决方案，无监督方法在实际应用中更具普适性。

authors_text: Fares Bougourzi,Fadi Dornaika,Amir Nakib,Cosimo Distante,Abdelmalik Taleb-Ahmed
title: 2D and 3D CNN-Based Fusion Approach for COVID-19 Severity Prediction from 3D CT-Scans
title_zh: 基于 2D 和 3D CNN 的融合方法，通过 3D CT 扫描预测 COVID-19 严重程度
comment: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2206.15431
comments: arXiv admin note: substantial text overlap with arXiv:2206.15431
abstract: Since the appearance of Covid-19 in late 2019, Covid-19 has become an active research topic for the artificial intelligence (AI) community. One of the most interesting AI topics is Covid-19 analysis of medical imaging. CT-scan imaging is the most informative tool about this disease. This work is part of the 3nd COV19D competition for Covid-19 Severity Prediction. In order to deal with the big gap between the validation and test results that were shown in the previous version of this competition, we proposed to combine the prediction of 2D and 3D CNN predictions. For the 2D CNN approach, we propose 2B-InceptResnet architecture which consists of two paths for segmented lungs and infection of all slices of the input CT-scan, respectively. Each path consists of ConvLayer and Inception-ResNet pretrained model on ImageNet. For the 3D CNN approach, we propose hybrid-DeCoVNet architecture which consists of four blocks: Stem, four 3D-ResNet layers, Classification Head and Decision layer. Our proposed approaches outperformed the baseline approach in the validation data of the 3nd COV19D competition for Covid-19 Severity Prediction by 36%.
summary: Since the appearance of Covid-19 in late 2019, Covid-19 has become an active research topic for the artificial intelligence (AI) community. One of the most interesting AI topics is Covid-19 analysis of medical imaging. CT-scan imaging is the most informative tool about this disease. This work is part of the 3nd COV19D competition for Covid-19 Severity Prediction. In order to deal with the big gap between the validation and test results that were shown in the previous version of this competition, we proposed to combine the prediction of 2D and 3D CNN predictions. For the 2D CNN approach, we propose 2B-InceptResnet architecture which consists of two paths for segmented lungs and infection of all slices of the input CT-scan, respectively. Each path consists of ConvLayer and Inception-ResNet pretrained model on ImageNet. For the 3D CNN approach, we propose hybrid-DeCoVNet architecture which consists of four blocks: Stem, four 3D-ResNet layers, Classification Head and Decision layer. Our proposed approaches outperformed the baseline approach in the validation data of the 3nd COV19D competition for Covid-19 Severity Prediction by 36%.
abstract_zh: 自 2019 年底 Covid-19 出现以来，Covid-19 已成为人工智能 (AI) 界的一个活跃研究课题。最有趣的人工智能主题之一是 Covid-19 医学成像分析。 CT 扫描成像是有关这种疾病的信息最丰富的工具。这项工作是第三届 COV19D Covid-19 严重程度预测竞赛的一部分。为了解决本次比赛之前版本中验证和测试结果之间的巨大差距，我们提出将 2D 和 3D CNN 预测结合起来。对于 2D CNN 方法，我们提出了 2B-InceptResnet 架构，该架构由分别用于分段肺部和输入 CT 扫描的所有切片的感染的两条路径组成。每条路径均由 ConvLayer 和 ImageNet 上的 Inception-ResNet 预训练模型组成。对于 3D CNN 方法，我们提出了混合 DeCoVNet 架构，该架构由四个块组成：Stem、四个 3D-ResNet 层、分类头和决策层。在第三届 COV19D Covid-19 严重程度预测竞赛的验证数据中，我们提出的方法比基线方法高出 36%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合2D和3D卷积神经网络（CNN）的融合方法，用于从3D CT扫描中预测COVID-19的严重程度。
AI.motivation: 为了解决之前竞赛版本中验证集与测试集结果差距较大的问题，作者提出通过融合2D和3D CNN预测结果来提高模型性能。
AI.method: 2D CNN采用双路径2B-InceptResnet架构，分别处理分割后的肺部和感染区域；3D CNN设计混合型DeCoVNet架构，包含Stem层、四层3D-ResNet、分类头和决策层。最终通过融合两种模型预测结果进行严重程度判断。
AI.result: 在第3届COV19D竞赛的验证数据中，该方法相比基线模型性能提升了36%。
AI.conclusion: 融合2D和3D CNN的方法能有效提升COVID-19严重程度预测的准确性，为医学影像分析提供了新的技术思路。

search_text: 2D and 3D CNN-Based Fusion Approach for COVID-19 Severity Prediction from 3D CT-Scans 基于 2D 和 3D CNN 的融合方法，通过 3D CT 扫描预测 COVID-19 严重程度 Since the appearance of Covid-19 in late 2019, Covid-19 has become an active research topic for the artificial intelligence (AI) community. One of the most interesting AI topics is Covid-19 analysis of medical imaging. CT-scan imaging is the most informative tool about this disease. This work is part of the 3nd COV19D competition for Covid-19 Severity Prediction. In order to deal with the big gap between the validation and test results that were shown in the previous version of this competition, we proposed to combine the prediction of 2D and 3D CNN predictions. For the 2D CNN approach, we propose 2B-InceptResnet architecture which consists of two paths for segmented lungs and infection of all slices of the input CT-scan, respectively. Each path consists of ConvLayer and Inception-ResNet pretrained model on ImageNet. For the 3D CNN approach, we propose hybrid-DeCoVNet architecture which consists of four blocks: Stem, four 3D-ResNet layers, Classification Head and Decision layer. Our proposed approaches outperformed the baseline approach in the validation data of the 3nd COV19D competition for Covid-19 Severity Prediction by 36%. 自 2019 年底 Covid-19 出现以来，Covid-19 已成为人工智能 (AI) 界的一个活跃研究课题。最有趣的人工智能主题之一是 Covid-19 医学成像分析。 CT 扫描成像是有关这种疾病的信息最丰富的工具。这项工作是第三届 COV19D Covid-19 严重程度预测竞赛的一部分。为了解决本次比赛之前版本中验证和测试结果之间的巨大差距，我们提出将 2D 和 3D CNN 预测结合起来。对于 2D CNN 方法，我们提出了 2B-InceptResnet 架构，该架构由分别用于分段肺部和输入 CT 扫描的所有切片的感染的两条路径组成。每条路径均由 ConvLayer 和 ImageNet 上的 Inception-ResNet 预训练模型组成。对于 3D CNN 方法，我们提出了混合 DeCoVNet 架构，该架构由四个块组成：Stem、四个 3D-ResNet 层、分类头和决策层。在第三届 COV19D Covid-19 严重程度预测竞赛的验证数据中，我们提出的方法比基线方法高出 36%。 本文提出了一种结合2D和3D卷积神经网络（CNN）的融合方法，用于从3D CT扫描中预测COVID-19的严重程度。 Fares Bougourzi,Fadi Dornaika,Amir Nakib,Cosimo Distante,Abdelmalik Taleb-Ahmed eess.IV,cs.CV

authors_text: Taras Kucherenko,Pieter Wolfert,Youngwoo Yoon,Carla Viegas,Teodor Nikolov,Mihail Tsakov,Gustav Eje Henter
title: Evaluating gesture generation in a large-scale open challenge: The GENEA Challenge 2022
title_zh: 在大规模公开挑战中评估手势生成：GENEA 挑战 2022
comment: The first three authors made equal contributions and share joint first authorship. Accepted for publication in the ACM Transactions on Graphics (TOG).Please see https://youngwoo-yoon.github.io/GENEAchallenge2022/ for all challenge materials. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2208.10441
comments: The first three authors made equal contributions and share joint first authorship. Accepted for publication in the ACM Transactions on Graphics (TOG).Please see https://youngwoo-yoon.github.io/GENEAchallenge2022/ for all challenge materials. arXiv admin note: text overlap with arXiv:2208.10441
abstract: This paper reports on the second GENEA Challenge to benchmark data-driven automatic co-speech gesture generation. Participating teams used the same speech and motion dataset to build gesture-generation systems. Motion generated by all these systems was rendered to video using a standardised visualisation pipeline and evaluated in several large, crowdsourced user studies. Unlike when comparing different research papers, differences in results are here only due to differences between methods, enabling direct comparison between systems. The dataset was based on 18 hours of full-body motion capture, including fingers, of different persons engaging in a dyadic conversation. Ten teams participated in the challenge across two tiers: full-body and upper-body gesticulation. For each tier, we evaluated both the human-likeness of the gesture motion and its appropriateness for the specific speech signal. Our evaluations decouple human-likeness from gesture appropriateness, which has been a difficult problem in the field. The evaluation results show some synthetic gesture conditions being rated as significantly more human-like than 3D human motion capture. To the best of our knowledge, this has not been demonstrated before. On the other hand, all synthetic motion is found to be vastly less appropriate for the speech than the original motion-capture recordings. We also find that conventional objective metrics do not correlate well with subjective human-likeness ratings in this large evaluation. The one exception is the Fréchet gesture distance (FGD), which achieves a Kendall's tau rank correlation of around $-0.5$. Based on the challenge results we formulate numerous recommendations for system building and evaluation.

Participating teams used the same speech and motion dataset to build gesture-generation systems. Motion generated by all these systems was rendered to video using a standardised visualisation pipeline and evaluated in several large, crowdsourced user studies. Unlike when comparing different research papers, differences in results are here only due to differences between methods, enabling direct comparison between systems. The dataset was based on 18 hours of full-body motion capture, including fingers, of different persons engaging in a dyadic conversation. Ten teams participated in the challenge across two tiers: full-body and upper-body gesticulation. For each tier, we evaluated both the human-likeness of the gesture motion and its appropriateness for the specific speech signal. Our evaluations decouple human-likeness from gesture appropriateness, which has been a difficult problem in the field. The evaluation results show some synthetic gesture conditions being rated as significantly more human-like than 3D human motion capture. To the best of our knowledge, this has not been demonstrated before. On the other hand, all synthetic motion is found to be vastly less appropriate for the speech than the original motion-capture recordings. We also find that conventional objective metrics do not correlate well with subjective human-likeness ratings in this large evaluation. The one exception is the Fréchet gesture distance (FGD), which achieves a Kendall's tau rank correlation of around $-0.5$. Based on the challenge results we formulate numerous recommendations for system building and evaluation.
abstract_zh: 本文报告了第二届 GEEA 挑战赛，以对数据驱动的自动协同语音手势生成进行基准测试。参与团队使用相同的语音和动作数据集来构建手势生成系统。所有这些系统生成的运动都使用标准化可视化管道呈现为视频，并在几项大型众包用户研究中进行评估。与比较不同研究论文不同，这里的结果差异仅是由于方法之间的差异，可以在系统之间进行直接比较。该数据集基于对参与二元对话的不同人 18 小时的全身动作捕捉（包括手指）。十支队伍参加了全身手势和上半身手势两个级别的挑战。对于每一层，我们评估了手势运动的人类相似性及其对特定语音信号的适当性。我们的评估将人类相似性与手势适当性脱钩，这一直是该领域的一个难题。评估结果显示，一些合成手势条件被评为比 3D 人体动作捕捉更接近人类。据我们所知，这之前尚未得到证实。另一方面，我们发现所有合成动作都比原始动作捕捉录音更不适合语音。我们还发现，在这次大型评估中，传统的客观指标与主观的人类相似度评级并没有很好的相关性。唯一的例外是 Fréchet 手势距离 (FGD)，它实现了大约 $-0.5$ 的 Kendall tau 等级相关性。根据挑战结果，我们为系统构建和评估制定了大量建议。

AI.tldr: 该论文报告了GENEA挑战赛2022的结果，评估了基于语音驱动的共情手势生成系统的性能。
AI.motivation: 通过统一数据集和评估流程，消除方法比较中的干扰因素，推动手势生成技术的客观评价。
AI.method: 使用18小时全身动作捕捉数据集，分全身和上半身两个层级，通过标准化可视化管道生成视频，并进行大规模众包用户研究评估拟人度和动作合理性。
AI.result: 合成手势在拟人度评分上部分超越真实动作捕捉数据，但与语音的适配性显著不足；Fréchet手势距离（FGD）是唯一与主观评分相关性较高的客观指标（Kendall's tau约-0.5）。
AI.conclusion: 提出系统构建与评估的多项建议，强调需平衡拟人度与语义适配性，并重视FGD等新型评价指标的应用。

authors_text: Hui Zhang,Zheng Wang,Dan Zeng,Zuxuan Wu,Yu-Gang Jiang
title: DiffusionAD: Norm-guided One-step Denoising Diffusion for Anomaly Detection
title_zh: DiffusionAD：规范引导的一步去噪扩散用于异常检测
abstract: Anomaly detection has garnered extensive applications in real industrial manufacturing due to its remarkable effectiveness and efficiency. However, previous generative-based models have been limited by suboptimal reconstruction quality, hampering their overall performance. We introduce DiffusionAD, a novel anomaly detection pipeline comprising a reconstruction sub-network and a segmentation sub-network. A fundamental enhancement lies in our reformulation of the reconstruction process using a diffusion model into a noise-to-norm paradigm. Here, the anomalous region loses its distinctive features after being disturbed by Gaussian noise and is subsequently reconstructed into an anomaly-free one. Afterward, the segmentation sub-network predicts pixel-level anomaly scores based on the similarities and discrepancies between the input image and its anomaly-free reconstruction. Additionally, given the substantial decrease in inference speed due to the iterative denoising nature of diffusion models, we revisit the denoising process and introduce a rapid one-step denoising paradigm. This paradigm achieves hundreds of times acceleration while preserving comparable reconstruction quality. Furthermore, considering the diversity in the manifestation of anomalies, we propose a norm-guided paradigm to integrate the benefits of multiple noise scales, enhancing the fidelity of reconstructions. Comprehensive evaluations on four standard and challenging benchmarks reveal that DiffusionAD outperforms current state-of-the-art approaches and achieves comparable inference speed, demonstrating the effectiveness and broad applicability of the proposed pipeline. Code is released at https://github.com/HuiZhang0812/DiffusionAD

Comprehensive evaluations on four standard and challenging benchmarks reveal that DiffusionAD outperforms current state-of-the-art approaches and achieves comparable inference speed, demonstrating the effectiveness and broad applicability of the proposed pipeline. Code is released at https://github.com/HuiZhang0812/DiffusionAD
abstract_zh: 异常检测因其显着的有效性和效率而在实际工业制造中获得了广泛的应用。然而，以前的基于生成的模型受到重建质量次优的限制，从而影响了它们的整体性能。我们引入了 DiffusionAD，一种新颖的异常检测管道，包括重建子网络和分割子网络。一个根本性的增强在于我们使用扩散模型将重建过程重新表述为噪声规范范式。在这里，异常区域在受到高斯噪声的干扰后失去了其独特的特征，随后被重建为无异常区域。然后，分割子网络根据输入图像与其无异常重建之间的相似性和差异来预测像素级异常分数。此外，考虑到由于扩散模型的迭代去噪性质而导致推理速度大幅下降，我们重新审视去噪过程并引入一种快速的一步去噪范例。该范例实现了数百倍的加速，同时保持了可比的重建质量。此外，考虑到异常表现的多样性，我们提出了一种规范引导范式来整合多个噪声尺度的优点，提高重建的保真度。对四个标准和具有挑战性的基准的综合评估表明，DiffusionAD 优于当前最先进的方法，并实现了可比的推理速度，证明了所提出的管道的有效性和广泛适用性。代码发布于https://github.com/HuiZhang0812/DiffusionAD

AI.tldr: 本文提出DiffusionAD，一种基于扩散模型的异常检测方法，通过重构和分割子网络实现高效异常检测。
AI.motivation: 现有生成模型因重构质量不足限制了异常检测性能，且扩散模型推理速度慢，需改进以提升效率和准确性。
AI.method: 设计包含重构子网络和分割子网络的框架，将扩散模型重构过程转化为噪声到规范范式，并引入一步去噪和规范引导机制以提升速度与重建质量。
AI.result: 在四个基准数据集上优于现有最先进方法，推理速度提升数百倍，验证了方法的有效性和广泛适用性。
AI.conclusion: DiffusionAD通过创新性架构和优化策略，在保持高重建质量的同时显著提升异常检测效率，为工业应用提供新思路。

search_text: DiffusionAD: Norm-guided One-step Denoising Diffusion for Anomaly Detection DiffusionAD：规范引导的一步去噪扩散用于异常检测 Anomaly detection has garnered extensive applications in real industrial manufacturing due to its remarkable effectiveness and efficiency. However, previous generative-based models have been limited by suboptimal reconstruction quality, hampering their overall performance. We introduce DiffusionAD, a novel anomaly detection pipeline comprising a reconstruction sub-network and a segmentation sub-network. A fundamental enhancement lies in our reformulation of the reconstruction process using a diffusion model into a noise-to-norm paradigm. Here, the anomalous region loses its distinctive features after being disturbed by Gaussian noise and is subsequently reconstructed into an anomaly-free one. Afterward, the segmentation sub-network predicts pixel-level anomaly scores based on the similarities and discrepancies between the input image and its anomaly-free reconstruction. Additionally, given the substantial decrease in inference speed due to the iterative denoising nature of diffusion models, we revisit the denoising process and introduce a rapid one-step denoising paradigm. This paradigm achieves hundreds of times acceleration while preserving comparable reconstruction quality. Furthermore, considering the diversity in the manifestation of anomalies, we propose a norm-guided paradigm to integrate the benefits of multiple noise scales, enhancing the fidelity of reconstructions. Comprehensive evaluations on four standard and challenging benchmarks reveal that DiffusionAD outperforms current state-of-the-art approaches and achieves comparable inference speed, demonstrating the effectiveness and broad applicability of the proposed pipeline. Code is released at https://github.com/HuiZhang0812/DiffusionAD 异常检测因其显着的有效性和效率而在实际工业制造中获得了广泛的应用。然而，以前的基于生成的模型受到重建质量次优的限制，从而影响了它们的整体性能。我们引入了 DiffusionAD，一种新颖的异常检测管道，包括重建子网络和分割子网络。一个根本性的增强在于我们使用扩散模型将重建过程重新表述为噪声规范范式。在这里，异常区域在受到高斯噪声的干扰后失去了其独特的特征，随后被重建为无异常区域。然后，分割子网络根据输入图像与其无异常重建之间的相似性和差异来预测像素级异常分数。此外，考虑到由于扩散模型的迭代去噪性质而导致推理速度大幅下降，我们重新审视去噪过程并引入一种快速的一步去噪范例。该范例实现了数百倍的加速，同时保持了可比的重建质量。此外，考虑到异常表现的多样性，我们提出了一种规范引导范式来整合多个噪声尺度的优点，提高重建的保真度。对四个标准和具有挑战性的基准的综合评估表明，DiffusionAD 优于当前最先进的方法，并实现了可比的推理速度，证明了所提出的管道的有效性和广泛适用性。代码发布于https://github.com/HuiZhang0812/DiffusionAD 本文提出DiffusionAD，一种基于扩散模型的异常检测方法，通过重构和分割子网络实现高效异常检测。 Hui Zhang,Zheng Wang,Dan Zeng,Zuxuan Wu,Yu-Gang Jiang cs.CV

authors_text: Himesh Nandani,Mootez Saad,Tushar Sharma
title: DACOS-A Manually Annotated Dataset of Code Smells
title_zh: DACOS-手动注释的代码味道数据集
abstract: Researchers apply machine-learning techniques for code smell detection to counter the subjectivity of many code smells. Such approaches need a large, manually annotated dataset for training and benchmarking. Existing literature offers a few datasets; however, they are small in size and, more importantly, do not focus on the subjective code snippets. In this paper, we present DACOS, a manually annotated dataset containing 10,267 annotations for 5,192 code snippets. The dataset targets three kinds of code smells at different granularity: multifaceted abstraction, complex method, and long parameter list. The dataset is created in two phases. The first phase helps us identify the code snippets that are potentially subjective by determining the thresholds of metrics used to detect a smell. The second phase collects annotations for potentially subjective snippets. We also offer an extended dataset DACOSX that includes definitely benign and definitely smelly snippets by using the thresholds identified in the first phase. We have developed TagMan, a web application to help annotators view and mark the snippets one-by-one and record the provided annotations. We make the datasets and the web application accessible publicly. This dataset will help researchers working on smell detection techniques to build relevant and context-aware machine-learning models.
summary: Researchers apply machine-learning techniques for code smell detection to counter the subjectivity of many code smells. Such approaches need a large, manually annotated dataset for training and benchmarking. Existing literature offers a few datasets; however, they are small in size and, more importantly, do not focus on the subjective code snippets. In this paper, we present DACOS, a manually annotated dataset containing 10,267 annotations for 5,192 code snippets. The dataset targets three kinds of code smells at different granularity: multifaceted abstraction, complex method, and long parameter list. The dataset is created in two phases. The first phase helps us identify the code snippets that are potentially subjective by determining the thresholds of metrics used to detect a smell. The second phase collects annotations for potentially subjective snippets. We also offer an extended dataset DACOSX that includes definitely benign and definitely smelly snippets by using the thresholds identified in the first phase. We have developed TagMan, a web application to help annotators view and mark the snippets one-by-one and record the provided annotations. We make the datasets and the web application accessible publicly. This dataset will help researchers working on smell detection techniques to build relevant and context-aware machine-learning models.
abstract_zh: 研究人员应用机器学习技术进行代码异味检测，以对抗许多代码异味的主观性。此类方法需要大量手动注释的数据集来进行训练和基准测试。现有文献提供了一些数据集；然而，它们的尺寸很小，更重要的是，它们不关注主观代码片段。在本文中，我们提出了 DACOS，这是一个手动注释的数据集，包含 5,192 个代码片段的 10,267 个注释。该数据集针对三种不同粒度的代码味道：多方面抽象、复杂方法和长参数列表。数据集分两个阶段创建。第一阶段通过确定用于检测气味的指标阈值来帮助我们识别潜在主观的代码片段。第二阶段收集潜在主观片段的注释。我们还提供了一个扩展数据集 DACOSX，其中通过使用第一阶段确定的阈值来包含绝对良性和绝对有臭的片段。我们开发了 TagMan，这是一个 Web 应用程序，可以帮助注释者逐一查看和标记片段并记录所提供的注释。我们公开提供数据集和网络应用程序。该数据集将帮助研究气味检测技术的研究人员构建相关且上下文感知的机器学习模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DACOS数据集，包含10,267个手动标注的代码异味片段，用于支持机器学习驱动的代码异味检测研究。
AI.motivation: 现有代码异味检测数据集规模小且缺乏对主观性代码片段的聚焦，难以支撑机器学习模型的训练与评估。
AI.method: 分两阶段构建数据集：第一阶段通过指标阈值筛选潜在主观性代码片段，第二阶段使用TagMan工具进行人工标注，并生成包含确定性样本的DACOSX扩展集。
AI.result: 发布DACOS（5,192个片段）和DACOSX数据集，配套开源标注工具TagMan，所有资源向公众开放。
AI.conclusion: 该数据集为研究者开发上下文感知的代码异味检测模型提供了高质量基准，推动主观性代码异味的自动化识别研究。

Such approaches need a large, manually annotated dataset for training and benchmarking. Existing literature offers a few datasets; however, they are small in size and, more importantly, do not focus on the subjective code snippets. In this paper, we present DACOS, a manually annotated dataset containing 10,267 annotations for 5,192 code snippets. The dataset targets three kinds of code smells at different granularity: multifaceted abstraction, complex method, and long parameter list. The dataset is created in two phases. The second phase collects annotations for potentially subjective snippets. authors_text: Choubo Ding,Guansong Pang
title: Improving Out-of-Distribution Detection with Disentangled Foreground and Background Features
title_zh: 通过分离前景和背景特征来改进分布外检测
abstract: Detecting out-of-distribution (OOD) inputs is a principal task for ensuring the safety of deploying deep-neural-network classifiers in open-set scenarios. OOD samples can be drawn from arbitrary distributions and exhibit deviations from in-distribution (ID) data in various dimensions, such as foreground features (e.g., objects in CIFAR100 images vs. those in CIFAR10 images) and background features (e.g., textural images vs. objects in CIFAR10). Existing methods can confound foreground and background features in training, failing to utilize the background features for OOD detection. This paper considers the importance of feature disentanglement in out-of-distribution detection and proposes the simultaneous exploitation of both foreground and background features to support the detection of OOD inputs in in out-of-distribution detection. To this end, we propose a novel framework that first disentangles foreground and background features from ID training samples via a dense prediction approach, and then learns a new classifier that can evaluate the OOD scores of test images from both foreground and background features. It is a generic framework that allows for a seamless combination with various existing OOD detection methods. Extensive experiments show that our approach 1) can substantially enhance the performance of four different state-of-the-art (SotA) OOD detection methods on multiple widely-used OOD datasets with diverse background features, and 2) achieves new SotA performance on these benchmarks.
summary: Detecting out-of-distribution (OOD) inputs is a principal task for ensuring the safety of deploying deep-neural-network classifiers in open-set scenarios. OOD samples can be drawn from arbitrary distributions and exhibit deviations from in-distribution (ID) data in various dimensions, such as foreground features (e.g., objects in CIFAR100 images vs. those in CIFAR10 images) and background features (e.g., textural images vs. objects in CIFAR10). Existing methods can confound foreground and background features in training, failing to utilize the background features for OOD detection. This paper considers the importance of feature disentanglement in out-of-distribution detection and proposes the simultaneous exploitation of both foreground and background features to support the detection of OOD inputs in in out-of-distribution detection. To this end, we propose a novel framework that first disentangles foreground and background features from ID training samples via a dense prediction approach, and then learns a new classifier that can evaluate the OOD scores of test images from both foreground and background features. It is a generic framework that allows for a seamless combination with various existing OOD detection methods. Extensive experiments show that our approach 1) can substantially enhance the performance of four different state-of-the-art (SotA) OOD detection methods on multiple widely-used OOD datasets with diverse background features, and 2) achieves new SotA performance on these benchmarks.
abstract_zh: 检测分布外（OOD）输入是确保在开放集场景中部署深度神经网络分类器安全的一项主要任务。 OOD 样本可以从任意分布中提取，并在各个维度上表现出与分布内 (ID) 数据的偏差，例如前景特征（例如，CIFAR100 图像中的对象与 CIFAR10 图像中的对象）和背景特征（例如，纹理图像与 CIFAR10 中的对象）。现有方法可能会在训练中混淆前景和背景特征，无法利用背景特征进行 OOD 检测。本文考虑了特征分离在分布外检测中的重要性，并提出同时利用前景和背景特征来支持分布外检测中 OOD 输入的检测。为此，我们提出了一种新颖的框架，首先通过密集预测方法从 ID 训练样本中分离出前景和背景特征，然后学习一个新的分类器，可以根据前景和背景特征评估测试图像的 OOD 分数。它是一个通用框架，可以与各种现有的 OOD 检测方法无缝结合。大量实验表明，我们的方法 1) 可以显着增强四种不同的最先进 (SotA) OOD 检测方法在具有不同背景特征的多个广泛使用的 OOD 数据集上的性能，2) 在这些基准上实现新的 SotA 性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通过解耦前景和背景特征来提升分布外检测性能的新框架。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在训练过程中混淆了前景和背景特征，未能有效利用背景特征进行分布外检测，导致检测性能受限。
AI.method: 通过密集预测方法从训练样本中解耦前景和背景特征，再训练一个能同时利用两者特征评估测试图像分布外分数的分类器。
AI.result: 在多个具有多样背景特征的基准数据集上显著提升四种最先进方法的性能，并取得新的最先进结果。
AI.conclusion: 解耦利用前景与背景特征能有效提升分布外检测能力，该框架具有通用性且可与现有方法无缝结合。

search_text: Improving Out-of-Distribution Detection with Disentangled Foreground and Background Features 通过分离前景和背景特征来改进分布外检测 Detecting out-of-distribution (OOD) inputs is a principal task for ensuring the safety of deploying deep-neural-network classifiers in open-set scenarios. OOD samples can be drawn from arbitrary distributions and exhibit deviations from in-distribution (ID) data in various dimensions, such as foreground features (e.g., objects in CIFAR100 images vs. those in CIFAR10 images) and background features (e.g., textural images vs. objects in CIFAR10). Existing methods can confound foreground and background features in training, failing to utilize the background features for OOD detection. This paper considers the importance of feature disentanglement in out-of-distribution detection and proposes the simultaneous exploitation of both foreground and background features to support the detection of OOD inputs in in out-of-distribution detection. To this end, we propose a novel framework that first disentangles foreground and background features from ID training samples via a dense prediction approach, and then learns a new classifier that can evaluate the OOD scores of test images from both foreground and background features. It is a generic framework that allows for a seamless combination with various existing OOD detection methods. Extensive experiments show that our approach 1) can substantially enhance the performance of four different state-of-the-art (SotA) OOD detection methods on multiple widely-used OOD datasets with diverse background features, and 2) achieves new SotA performance on these benchmarks. 检测分布外（OOD）输入是确保在开放集场景中部署深度神经网络分类器安全的一项主要任务。 OOD 样本可以从任意分布中提取，并在各个维度上表现出与分布内 (ID) 数据的偏差，例如前景特征（例如，CIFAR100 图像中的对象与 CIFAR10 图像中的对象）和背景特征（例如，纹理图像与 CIFAR10 中的对象）。现有方法可能会在训练中混淆前景和背景特征，无法利用背景特征进行 OOD 检测。本文考虑了特征分离在分布外检测中的重要性，并提出同时利用前景和背景特征来支持分布外检测中 OOD 输入的检测。为此，我们提出了一种新颖的框架，首先通过密集预测方法从 ID 训练样本中分离出前景和背景特征，然后学习一个新的分类器，可以根据前景和背景特征评估测试图像的 OOD 分数。它是一个通用框架，可以与各种现有的 OOD 检测方法无缝结合。大量实验表明，我们的方法 1) 可以显着增强四种不同的最先进 (SotA) OOD 检测方法在具有不同背景特征的多个广泛使用的 OOD 数据集上的性能，2) 在这些基准上实现新的 SotA 性能。 本文提出一种通过解耦前景和背景特征来提升分布外检测性能的新框架。 Choubo Ding,Guansong Pang cs.CV

authors_text: Matthew Burtell,Thomas Woodside
title: Artificial Influence: An Analysis Of AI-Driven Persuasion
title_zh: 人工影响：人工智能驱动的说服力分析
abstract: Persuasion is a key aspect of what it means to be human, and is central to business, politics, and other endeavors. Advancements in artificial intelligence (AI) have produced AI systems that are capable of persuading humans to buy products, watch videos, click on search results, and more. Even systems that are not explicitly designed to persuade may do so in practice. In the future, increasingly anthropomorphic AI systems may form ongoing relationships with users, increasing their persuasive power. This paper investigates the uncertain future of persuasive AI systems. We examine ways that AI could qualitatively alter our relationship to and views regarding persuasion by shifting the balance of persuasive power, allowing personalized persuasion to be deployed at scale, powering misinformation campaigns, and changing the way humans can shape their own discourse. We consider ways AI-driven persuasion could differ from human-driven persuasion. We warn that ubiquitous highlypersuasive AI systems could alter our information environment so significantly so as to contribute to a loss of human control of our own future. In response, we examine several potential responses to AI-driven persuasion: prohibition, identification of AI agents, truthful AI, and legal remedies. We conclude that none of these solutions will be airtight, and that individuals and governments will need to take active steps to guard against the most pernicious effects of persuasive AI.

We conclude that none of these solutions will be airtight, and that individuals and governments will need to take active steps to guard against the most pernicious effects of persuasive AI.
abstract_zh: 说服是人类意义的一个关键方面，也是商业、政治和其他努力的核心。人工智能 (AI) 的进步已经产生了能够说服人类购买产品、观看视频、点击搜索结果等的人工智能系统。即使没有明确设计用于说服的系统在实践中也可能会这样做。未来，越来越拟人化的人工智能系统可能会与用户形成持续的关系，从而增强他们的说服力。本文研究了说服性人工智能系统的不确定未来。我们研究了人工智能如何通过改变说服力的平衡、允许大规模部署个性化说服、推动错误信息活动以及改变人类塑造自己话语的方式来定性地改变我们与说服的关系和看法。我们考虑人工智能驱动的说服与人类驱动的说服的不同之处。我们警告说，无处不在的高说服力人工智能系统可能会极大地改变我们的信息环境，从而导致人类失去对自己未来的控制。作为回应，我们研究了对人工智能驱动的说服的几种潜在反应：禁止、人工智能代理的识别、真实的人工智能和法律补救措施。我们的结论是，这些解决方案都不会是无懈可击的，个人和政府需要采取积极措施来防范说服性人工智能最有害的影响。

AI.tldr: 本文分析了人工智能驱动的说服技术的未来影响，并探讨了应对策略。
AI.motivation: 随着AI系统在商业、政治等领域的说服能力增强，人类对信息环境的控制可能被削弱，需提前预警并寻找解决方案。
AI.method: 通过分析AI说服的潜在影响（如权力失衡、规模化个性化、虚假信息传播），对比AI与人类说服的差异，并评估禁令、AI标识、法律等应对措施。
AI.result: 现有解决方案均存在局限性，无法完全遏制AI说服带来的负面影响。
AI.conclusion: 需个人与政府主动采取措施，以应对AI驱动的说服技术对人类未来的潜在威胁。

authors_text: Sara Rojas,Jesus Zarzar,Juan Camilo Perez,Artsiom Sanakoyeu,Ali Thabet,Albert Pumarola,Bernard Ghanem
title: Re-ReND: Real-time Rendering of NeRFs across Devices
title_zh: Re-ReND：跨设备实时渲染 NeRF
abstract: This paper proposes a novel approach for rendering a pre-trained Neural Radiance Field (NeRF) in real-time on resource-constrained devices. We introduce Re-ReND, a method enabling Real-time Rendering of NeRFs across Devices. Re-ReND is designed to achieve real-time performance by converting the NeRF into a representation that can be efficiently processed by standard graphics pipelines. The proposed method distills the NeRF by extracting the learned density into a mesh, while the learned color information is factorized into a set of matrices that represent the scene's light field. Factorization implies the field is queried via inexpensive MLP-free matrix multiplications, while using a light field allows rendering a pixel by querying the field a single time-as opposed to hundreds of queries when employing a radiance field. Since the proposed representation can be implemented using a fragment shader, it can be directly integrated with standard rasterization frameworks. Our flexible implementation can render a NeRF in real-time with low memory requirements and on a wide range of resource-constrained devices, including mobiles and AR/VR headsets. Notably, we find that Re-ReND can achieve over a 2.6-fold increase in rendering speed versus the state-of-the-art without perceptible losses in quality.
summary: This paper proposes a novel approach for rendering a pre-trained Neural Radiance Field (NeRF) in real-time on resource-constrained devices. We introduce Re-ReND, a method enabling Real-time Rendering of NeRFs across Devices. Re-ReND is designed to achieve real-time performance by converting the NeRF into a representation that can be efficiently processed by standard graphics pipelines. The proposed method distills the NeRF by extracting the learned density into a mesh, while the learned color information is factorized into a set of matrices that represent the scene's light field. Factorization implies the field is queried via inexpensive MLP-free matrix multiplications, while using a light field allows rendering a pixel by querying the field a single time-as opposed to hundreds of queries when employing a radiance field. Since the proposed representation can be implemented using a fragment shader, it can be directly integrated with standard rasterization frameworks. Our flexible implementation can render a NeRF in real-time with low memory requirements and on a wide range of resource-constrained devices, including mobiles and AR/VR headsets. Notably, we find that Re-ReND can achieve over a 2.6-fold increase in rendering speed versus the state-of-the-art without perceptible losses in quality.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种在资源受限的设备上实时渲染预先训练的神经辐射场（NeRF）的新方法。我们引入了 Re-ReND，这是一种跨设备实时渲染 NeRF 的方法。 Re-ReND 旨在通过将 NeRF 转换为可以由标准图形管道有效处理的表示来实现实时性能。所提出的方法通过将学习到的密度提取到网格中来提炼 NeRF，同时将学习到的颜色信息分解为一组表示场景光场的矩阵。因式分解意味着通过廉价的无 MLP 矩阵乘法来查询该场，而使用光场允许通过一次查询该场来渲染像素，而不是使用辐射场时进行数百次查询。由于所提出的表示可以使用片段着色器来实现，因此它可以直接与标准光栅化框架集成。我们灵活的实施可以以较低的内存要求在各种资源受限的设备（包括手机和 AR/VR 耳机）上实时渲染 NeRF。值得注意的是，我们发现 Re-ReND 的渲染速度比最先进的技术提高了 2.6 倍以上，而且质量没有明显损失。

AI.tldr: 本文提出Re-ReND方法，通过将神经辐射场（NeRF）转换为可被标准图形流水线高效处理的表示形式，实现在资源受限设备上的实时渲染。
AI.motivation: 传统NeRF方法在计算资源消耗大，难以在移动设备或AR/VR头显等资源受限设备上实现实时渲染，需寻找更高效的表示方式。
AI.method: 通过将NeRF的密度信息提取为网格，颜色信息分解为表示光场的矩阵，利用矩阵乘法替代MLP计算，并通过片段着色器实现与标准光栅化框架的集成。
AI.result: 在保持视觉质量无明显损失的前提下，渲染速度较现有最佳方法提升2.6倍，可在移动设备和AR/VR设备上实现低内存消耗的实时渲染。
AI.conclusion: Re-ReND通过优化NeRF表示形式和利用图形硬件加速，有效解决了在资源受限设备上实时渲染NeRF的难题。

tldr: 本文提出Re-ReND方法，通过将神经辐射场（NeRF）转换为可被标准图形流水线高效处理的表示形式，实现在资源受限设备上的实时渲染。
motivation: 传统NeRF方法在计算资源消耗大，难以在移动设备或AR/VR头显等资源受限设备上实现实时渲染，需寻找更高效的表示方式。
method: 通过将NeRF的密度信息提取为网格，颜色信息分解为表示光场的矩阵，利用矩阵乘法替代MLP计算，并通过片段着色器实现与标准光栅化框架的集成。
result: 在保持视觉质量无明显损失的前提下，渲染速度较现有最佳方法提升2.6倍，可在移动设备和AR/VR设备上实现低内存消耗的实时渲染。
conclusion: Re-ReND通过优化NeRF表示形式和利用图形硬件加速，有效解决了在资源受限设备上实时渲染NeRF的难题。
search_text: Re-ReND: Real-time Rendering of NeRFs across Devices Re-ReND：跨设备实时渲染 NeRF This paper proposes a novel approach for rendering a pre-trained Neural Radiance Field (NeRF) in real-time on resource-constrained devices. We introduce Re-ReND, a method enabling Real-time Rendering of NeRFs across Devices. Re-ReND is designed to achieve real-time performance by converting the NeRF into a representation that can be efficiently processed by standard graphics pipelines. The proposed method distills the NeRF by extracting the learned density into a mesh, while the learned color information is factorized into a set of matrices that represent the scene's light field. Factorization implies the field is queried via inexpensive MLP-free matrix multiplications, while using a light field allows rendering a pixel by querying the field a single time-as opposed to hundreds of queries when employing a radiance field. Since the proposed representation can be implemented using a fragment shader, it can be directly integrated with standard rasterization frameworks. Our flexible implementation can render a NeRF in real-time with low memory requirements and on a wide range of resource-constrained devices, including mobiles and AR/VR headsets. Notably, we find that Re-ReND can achieve over a 2.6-fold increase in rendering speed versus the state-of-the-art without perceptible losses in quality. 本文提出了一种在资源受限的设备上实时渲染预先训练的神经辐射场（NeRF）的新方法。我们引入了 Re-ReND，这是一种跨设备实时渲染 NeRF 的方法。 Re-ReND 旨在通过将 NeRF 转换为可以由标准图形管道有效处理的表示来实现实时性能。所提出的方法通过将学习到的密度提取到网格中来提炼 NeRF，同时将学习到的颜色信息分解为一组表示场景光场的矩阵。因式分解意味着通过廉价的无 MLP 矩阵乘法来查询该场，而使用光场允许通过一次查询该场来渲染像素，而不是使用辐射场时进行数百次查询。由于所提出的表示可以使用片段着色器来实现，因此它可以直接与标准光栅化框架集成。我们灵活的实施可以以较低的内存要求在各种资源受限的设备（包括手机和 AR/VR 耳机）上实时渲染 NeRF。值得注意的是，我们发现 Re-ReND 的渲染速度比最先进的技术提高了 2.6 倍以上，而且质量没有明显损失。 本文提出Re-ReND方法，通过将神经辐射场（NeRF）转换为可被标准图形流水线高效处理的表示形式，实现在资源受限设备上的实时渲染。 Sara Rojas,Jesus Zarzar,Juan Camilo Perez,Artsiom Sanakoyeu,Ali Thabet,Albert Pumarola,Bernard Ghanem cs.CV,cs.GR

authors_text: Shuyao Shang,Zhengyang Shan,Guangxing Liu,LunQian Wang,XingHua Wang,Zekai Zhang,Jinglin Zhang
title: ResDiff: Combining CNN and Diffusion Model for Image Super-Resolution
title_zh: ResDiff：结合 CNN 和扩散模型实现图像超分辨率
abstract: Adapting the Diffusion Probabilistic Model (DPM) for direct image super-resolution is wasteful, given that a simple Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) can recover the main low-frequency content. Therefore, we present ResDiff, a novel Diffusion Probabilistic Model based on Residual structure for Single Image Super-Resolution (SISR). ResDiff utilizes a combination of a CNN, which restores primary low-frequency components, and a DPM, which predicts the residual between the ground-truth image and the CNN predicted image. In contrast to the common diffusion-based methods that directly use LR images to guide the noise towards HR space, ResDiff utilizes the CNN's initial prediction to direct the noise towards the residual space between HR space and CNN-predicted space, which not only accelerates the generation process but also acquires superior sample quality. Additionally, a frequency-domain-based loss function for CNN is introduced to facilitate its restoration, and a frequency-domain guided diffusion is designed for DPM on behalf of predicting high-frequency details. The extensive experiments on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that ResDiff outperforms previous diffusion based methods in terms of shorter model convergence time, superior generation quality, and more diverse samples.
summary: Adapting the Diffusion Probabilistic Model (DPM) for direct image super-resolution is wasteful, given that a simple Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) can recover the main low-frequency content. Therefore, we present ResDiff, a novel Diffusion Probabilistic Model based on Residual structure for Single Image Super-Resolution (SISR). ResDiff utilizes a combination of a CNN, which restores primary low-frequency components, and a DPM, which predicts the residual between the ground-truth image and the CNN predicted image. In contrast to the common diffusion-based methods that directly use LR images to guide the noise towards HR space, ResDiff utilizes the CNN's initial prediction to direct the noise towards the residual space between HR space and CNN-predicted space, which not only accelerates the generation process but also acquires superior sample quality. Additionally, a frequency-domain-based loss function for CNN is introduced to facilitate its restoration, and a frequency-domain guided diffusion is designed for DPM on behalf of predicting high-frequency details. The extensive experiments on multiple benchmark datasets demonstrate that ResDiff outperforms previous diffusion based methods in terms of shorter model convergence time, superior generation quality, and more diverse samples.
abstract_zh: 考虑到简单的卷积神经网络（CNN）可以恢复主要低频内容，采用扩散概率模型（DPM）进行直接图像超分辨率是浪费的。因此，我们提出了 ResDiff，一种基于单图像超分辨率（SISR）残差结构的新型扩散概率模型。 ResDiff 结合了 CNN（恢复主要低频分量）和 DPM（预测真实图像与 CNN 预测图像之间的残差）。与常见的直接使用LR图像将噪声引导至HR空间的基于扩散的方法相比，ResDiff利用CNN的初始预测将噪声引导至HR空间和CNN预测空间之间的残差空间，这不仅加速了生成过程，而且获得了优异的样本质量。此外，为CNN引入了基于频域的损失函数以方便其恢复，并且为DPM设计了频域引导扩散来代表预测高频细节。对多个基准数据集的大量实验表明，ResDiff 在更短的模型收敛时间、卓越的生成质量和更多样化的样本方面优于以前的基于扩散的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出ResDiff，结合CNN与扩散模型进行图像超分辨率，通过CNN恢复低频内容，DPM预测残差以提升生成质量。
AI.motivation: 直接使用扩散模型进行超分辨率效率低，而CNN能有效恢复低频信息，需结合两者优势以提高效率和质量。
AI.method: ResDiff利用CNN恢复低频成分，DPM预测CNN输出与真实图像的残差；引入频域损失优化CNN，并设计频域引导扩散以恢复高频细节。
AI.result: 在多个基准数据集上，ResDiff相较现有扩散方法收敛更快，生成图像质量更优，样本多样性更高。
AI.conclusion: ResDiff通过分离低频与高频处理，有效结合CNN与扩散模型优势，在超分辨率任务中取得更优性能。
